message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: credentials-and-history.

Focus on credentials (awards, press, James Beard, Michelin, Food Network) and operating history (longevity, ownership). Try to REFUTE each claim. Look up each award independently — James Beard winners and nominees are listed in primary sources at jamesbeard.org. Michelin is listed at guide.michelin.com. Cross-check year-by-year.

Entity under review: Johnny Costa's Ristorante (Palm Springs)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp 599 reviews; Tripadvisor 650 reviews; Google rating ~4.6 (exact Google count [UNVERIFIED] — secondary sources cite 496–539 reviewers); OpenTable cited at ~2575 diner reviews in one source [UNVERIFIED specific number]
- Sentiment: mostly positive — strong fan base praises nostalgic atmosphere, signature dishes (Steak Sinatra, linguine clams, veal piccata), and attentive service; minority of recent reviews flag inconsistency, oversauced pasta, or 'overrated' value
- Longevity: Operating since circa 1976 (Desert Hot Springs → Cathedral City → current Palm Springs location at 440 S. Palm Canyon Dr.); family-owned with son Vince Costa as Head Chef — ~50 years, stable family ownership
- Credentials: ["Palm Springs Life Readers' Choice — 'Best Italian Restaurant' (multiple consecutive years; site/press references '10 consecutive years' [UNVERIFIED exact count])","OpenTable 2025 recognition badge displayed on site","Featured in Palm Springs Life '7 Must-Try Italian Restaurants Across Greater Palm Springs'","Featured in Visit Greater Palm Springs official tourism listings","Featured in regional press (PS Tribune) for Sinatra legacy"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces reliably for: 'best Italian restaurant Palm Springs', 'classic/old-school Italian Palm Springs', 'Sinatra restaurants Palm Springs', 'historic Palm Springs dining'. Does NOT consistently surface for generic 'date night Palm Springs' or 'fine dining Palm Springs' queries — those are dominated by Mister Parker's, 4 Saints, FARM, Bar Cecil, Copley's, The Tropicale. Weak presence in broader 'best restaurants Coachella Valley' queries despite tenure.
- Site structured data: Weak. Own website (johnnycostaspalmsprings.com) shows clean human-readable NAP (address 440 S. Palm Canyon Dr, phone 760-325-4556, hours Tues–Thurs 5–9:30, Fri–Sat 5–10, Sun–Mon closed) and dedicated menu/wine/dessert/drink pages — but no detectable JSON-LD schema.org markup, no Restaurant schema, no Open Graph tags observed in fetched HTML. Machine-readability for agents is minimal; relies on third-party aggregators (OpenTable, Tripadvisor, Yelp, Visit Greater Palm Springs) to broker structured signals.
- Visibility gap score: 6
- Convertibility tier: convertible

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "credentials-and-history"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the search results, here's the history of Johnny Costa's Ristorante in Palm Springs and its connection to Frank Sinatra:

## Origin and Founding

Johnny Costa, the founder, has been a chef and restaurant owner in the Palm Springs area since 1976. However, it's important to note that Johnny opened Johnny Costa's Ristorante in Desert Hot Springs, circa 1976, and then moved to Cathedral City before landing, permanently, in Palm Springs.

## Connection to Frank Sinatra

The restaurant was not founded by Frank Sinatra, but rather by Johnny Costa, who had a personal relationship with Sinatra. Johnny Costa was hired as Sous Chef and met Frank Sinatra where he learned the dish Frank Sinatra liked that was named Steak Sinatra. In the mid-70's Frank hired Johnny to work at his estate with his personal chef.

Frank Sinatra himself was a regular at this Italian restaurant, where the chairman's two favorite dishes – the steak Sinatra and linguine clams – are still on the menu. When Costa eventually moved to Palm Springs, Sinatra often joined him at Club Trinidad and later at Johnny's own restaurant.

## Legacy

The beloved Johnny Costa passed away on September 4, 2019 at age 87 and is survived by the Costa family who continue to run the restaurant and carry on the legacy of their dad. The restaurant remains a family-owned operation with Sinatra's influence still evident in the signature dishes on the menu.

content.content:
# Johnny Costa's Ristorante: Longevity, Awards & Family Legacy

**Longevity & Awards:**
The restaurant is marking its 49th season, having reopened August 29th, 2025. It has earned an impressive distinction: "Voted Best Italian Restaurant in Palm Springs by Palm Springs Life Readers the last 15 consecutive years."

**Costa Family Leadership:**
The Costa family continues the original proprietor's legacy. Head Chef Vince Costa (Johnny's son) leads the kitchen, while his brother John serves as General Manager. Family members Sarah Vertucci (Assistant Manager), cousin Chip Illiani, and brothers Nick and Tony Costa all contribute to operations.

**Historical Significance:**
The restaurant maintains legendary recipes from late chef-owner Johnny Costa, who served as Frank Sinatra's personal chef in the 1960s-70s. Signature dishes named after Sinatra—including Steak Sinatra and Clams Linguini—reflect this storied connection.

The combination of nearly five decades of continuous operation, sustained reader recognition, and multigenerational family stewardship demonstrates both the restaurant's staying power and its meaningful role in the local dining scene.

Based on the provided content:

**Opening Date:** Johnny Costa's Ristorante opened "circa 1976" in Desert Hot Springs, later relocating to Cathedral City and then to the current Palm Springs location.

**Current Management:** The restaurant is now run by Johnny's son, Head Chef Vince Costa, along with his brothers and cousins who assist in operations.

**Awards:** The page mentions a "Best of the Best 2020" award badge from what appears to be a local publication, but no specific mention of Palm Springs Life Readers Choice awards or consecutive years of recognition is provided in this content.

# Johnny Costa's Ristorante - Awards & Information

## Awards & Recognition
- **Palm Springs Life Readers Choice**: "Voted Best Italian Restaurant by Palm Springs Life Readers!" (specific consecutive years not mentioned)
- **OpenTable Badge**: 2025 OpenTable Diner's Choice Award (visible badge on homepage)

## Contact & Location
- **Address**: 440 S. Palm Canyon Drive, Palm Springs, CA 92262
- **Phone**: (760) 325-4556

## Operating Hours
- Tuesday–Thursday: 5 PM–9:30 PM
- Friday–Saturday: 5 PM–10 PM
- Sunday & Monday: Closed

## Additional Notable Detail
The establishment is described as "one of the few Italian restaurants in the area that serves fresh homemade dishes including pasta and their well known brick oven pizza's" with a dress code of casual resort wear requiring proper attire.

Based on the search results, here's what I found:

Johnny Costa, the founder-chef of Johnny Costa's Ristorante in Palm Springs, passed away in September 2019. He was a chef and restaurant owner for over 4 decades in the Palm Springs area, and opened his eponymous restaurant which started in Desert Hot Springs and then moved to Cathedral City before landing permanently in Palm Springs.

He is survived by his sons Vince, Tony, John Jr., Nick and daughter Michelle. Vince Costa is Johnny's son and current owner/chef of Johnny Costa's Ristorante.

Johnny Costa became friends with Frank Sinatra when he moved to Hollywood from New York in the 1960s, and later worked as Sinatra's personal chef at his desert home. Johnny Costa founded the restaurant and has been a chef and restaurant owner in the Palm Springs area since 1976.

content.text: Strong corroboration on history: 1976 founding, Desert Hot Springs → Cathedral City → Palm Springs, Vince Costa as son/Head Chef, founder died Sept 2019. Palm Springs Life Best Italian is confirmed but specific consecutive-year count varies (10 in one source, 13 in another, 15 in the Coachella Valley Weekly article). The "10 consecutive years" the assessment cited as [UNVERIFIED] appears too low — current sources say 15.

confirms: Operating since circa 1976 (Desert Hot Springs → Cathedral City → Palm Springs at 440 S. Palm Canyon Dr.) — confirmed by official history page, Palm Springs Life, PS Tribune, Coachella Valley Weekly (which marks the 2025 reopening as the 49th season, consistent with 1976 founding)

Family-owned with son Vince Costa as Head Chef — confirmed by official site and Coachella Valley Weekly; founder Johnny Costa died Sept 4, 2019 (Patch, KESQ), restaurant continued under Costa family (Vince chef, brother John GM, others assist)
Palm Springs Life Readers' Choice Best Italian Restaurant — confirmed as a real, recurring award (multiple sources including Palm Springs Life's own directory and the restaurant's site)
Tripadvisor 650 reviews — confirmed directly via Tripadvisor listing (4.4 stars, ranked #15 of 281 PS restaurants)
Yelp 599 reviews — confirmed via Yelp listing title
OpenTable 2025 Diner's Choice badge — confirmed displayed on official homepage
Featured in Palm Springs Life '7 Must-Try Italian Restaurants Across Greater Palm Springs' — confirmed via search results
Featured in Visit Greater Palm Springs official tourism listing — confirmed (visitgreaterpalmsprings.com listing exists)
Featured in PS Tribune for Sinatra legacy — confirmed (pstribune.com 2023 article)
Address 440 S. Palm Canyon Dr., phone 760-325-4556, hours Tues–Thurs 5–9:30, Fri–Sat 5–10, Sun–Mon closed — confirmed via official site
contradicts: Consecutive-year count for Palm Springs Life Best Italian: assessment flagged '10 consecutive years' as [UNVERIFIED]. Independent sources put it HIGHER, not lower — Coachella Valley Weekly (Aug 2025) states '15 consecutive years' tied to the 49th-season opening; other sources cite 13. The '10' figure understates the actual streak; current claim is closer to 15 consecutive years as of 2025.
flagged_as_unverified: Exact OpenTable review count (~2575) — one secondary search result returned that number but the OpenTable page itself timed out on fetch; could not independently corroborate the exact figure
Exact Google rating ~4.6 and review count (496–539 range) — not independently verified in this pass; no direct Google Business Profile fetch attempted
Sentiment characterization (mostly positive with minority of recent inconsistency complaints) — plausible given 4.4 Tripadvisor / Yelp scores but not granularly verified by reading recent reviews
Query-surface behavior (surfaces for 'best Italian Palm Springs' but not 'date night' / 'fine dining') — not tested in this verification lens
Structured-data absence (no JSON-LD / schema.org / Open Graph on own site) — not directly inspected in this pass; would need raw HTML fetch and parse
input.notes: History and family-ownership claims are strongly corroborated across multiple independent sources (official site, Palm Springs Life, PS Tribune, KESQ, Patch, Coachella Valley Weekly): 1976 founding, three-location lineage ending at 440 S. Palm Canyon Dr., founder Johnny Costa died Sept 4, 2019, succeeded by son Vince as Head Chef. The 2025 reopening as the '49th season' arithmetically aligns with the 1976 origin. Sinatra personal-chef relationship is consistently reported. No James Beard, Michelin, Food Network, or major national-press credentials are claimed and none surface in independent search — appropriately absent. The Palm Springs Life Best Italian award is real, but the assessment's tentative '10 consecutive years' underrepresents the publicly stated streak (15 per the restaurant's own 2025 press; 13 per another source). Worth tightening the claim to '~15 consecutive years (per 2025 press)' rather than '10'. Tripadvisor and Yelp review counts match exactly. OpenTable exact count (2575) remains uncorroborated due to fetch timeout.
